# Version 4.151.0 (Unreleased)
> **Performance and WASM updates**

## Highlights

SkiaSharp 4.151.0 ports hot-path colour conversions to managed code and updates WASM exception handling. SKColor-to-SKColorF and SKPMColor premultiply/unpremultiply now run entirely in managed integer math, eliminating P/Invoke overhead on these common paths. WASM support is refreshed with Emscripten 5.0.6 and the new exnref exception-handling model.

## Breaking Changes

*None in this preview line.*

## Engine

- **Upstream m151 bug fixes merged** — Picks up the latest round of upstream Skia chrome/m151 fixes, keeping the bundled engine aligned with Chrome milestone 151. ([#4489](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4489))
- **CPU raster regression corrected** — Restores SK_ENABLE_LEGACY_SHADERCONTEXT so CPU-rasterised surfaces produce correct output after the m151 sync. ([#4428](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4428))

## Bug Fixes

- **SKColor and SKColorF conversion ported to managed code** — SKColor-to-SKColorF conversion is now pure managed C# integer math, removing the P/Invoke round-trip on every colour conversion call. ([#4370](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4370))
- **SKPMColor premultiply/unpremultiply ported to managed code** — Single-value premultiply and unpremultiply operations are now handled entirely in managed integer math, avoiding native interop overhead. ([#4385](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4385))

## Platform

- **WASM updated to Emscripten 5.0.6 with exnref exception handling** — Rebuilds the WASM native libraries with Emscripten 5.0.6 and enables the new exnref-based exception handling model required by upcoming .NET WASM runtimes. ([#4487](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4487))
- **Preview-.NET WASM test job added** — A CI test job now validates SkiaSharp against the preview .NET WASM runtime, catching exception-handling regressions before they reach stable releases. ([#4488](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4488))
